title: Tunis 7-Day Heritage Tour
metaTitle: Tunis 7-Day Heritage Tour | Flights, Hotels & Tours Included
metaDescription: Book a 7-day trip to Tunis, Tunisia: round-trip flights, hotel, breakfast, transfers, and guided tours including Carthage archaeological site and Bardo National Museum. Prices from $1160 per person (estimate).
overview: Spend 7 days discovering Tunis, Tunisia — Mediterranean climate — hot, dry summers (highs around 33°C) and mild, wetter winters (highs around 15°C). March-May and September-November, for mild, comfortable temperatures. This itinerary blends Carthage archaeological site, Bardo National Museum, Medina of Tunis with free time to explore at your own pace, backed by round-trip flights, handpicked accommodation, and a dedicated travel consultant from enquiry through departure.
included: Round-trip economy-class airfare from your selected origin city (upgrade to premium economy or business available)
6 nights accommodation in your selected category (Budget, Mid-range, Premium, or Luxury)
Daily local transport as per the itinerary
Guided tours including Carthage archaeological site, Bardo National Museum, Medina of Tunis
All entrance fees for attractions and activities listed in the itinerary
Applicable taxes and OTA service fee
Dedicated travel consultant support from booking through departure
notIncluded: Visa fees and travel document costs (see Visa Information)
Travel insurance (strongly recommended, available as an add-on)
Lunches and dinners except where noted in the itinerary
Optional activities not listed in the day-by-day itinerary
Airline seat selection and baggage upgrades
Any fare difference for premium economy or business class upgrades

itinerary.title: Arrive in Tunis
itinerary.description: Land at Tunis-Carthage International Airport (TUN) and take your included private transfer to your hotel. After settling in, ease into the trip with a relaxed visit to Carthage archaeological site — Ruins of the ancient Phoenician-Roman city, including the Antonine Baths. Round off the evening with a welcome dinner introducing you to local flavors.

itinerary.title: Tunis: Bardo National Museum & Sidi Bou Said day visit
itinerary.description: Morning visit to Bardo National Museum — One of the world's finest Roman mosaic collections, housed in a former palace. In the afternoon, continue to Sidi Bou Said day visit — Iconic blue-and-white clifftop village overlooking the bay. Evening free to explore local restaurants and nightlife.

itinerary.title: Tunis: Dougga Roman ruins day trip & Medina of Tunis
itinerary.description: Morning visit to Dougga Roman ruins day trip — UNESCO-listed, remarkably intact Roman provincial town. In the afternoon, continue to Medina of Tunis — UNESCO-listed old city of covered souqs and historic mosques. Evening free to explore local restaurants and nightlife.

itinerary.title: Day Trip to Dougga Roman ruins
itinerary.description: Full-day excursion to Dougga Roman ruins, a popular side trip from Tunis. Return to Tunis in the evening with time free to explore on your own.
activities: Guided day trip: Dougga Roman ruins

itinerary.title: Leisure Day & Optional Activities
itinerary.description: A lighter day to recharge, shop, or add an optional experience. Popular choices include Dougga Roman ruins — your consultant can book add-ons like this on request.

itinerary.title: Tunis: Sidi Bou Said village streets & Avenue Habib Bourguiba
itinerary.description: Morning visit to Sidi Bou Said village streets — Free to wander the blue-and-white cobblestone lanes. In the afternoon, continue to Avenue Habib Bourguiba — Tunis's grand central boulevard, lined with cafés. Evening free to explore local restaurants and nightlife.

itinerary.title: Departure Day
itinerary.description: Enjoy a final morning at leisure — perhaps one last visit to Zitouna Mosque courtyard or some souvenir shopping — before your private transfer to TUN for your departure flight.

travelTips: Take the TGM light rail to Sidi Bou Said and Carthage rather than a taxi — it's cheap, scenic, and avoids traffic.
Bargain in the Medina souqs — initial asking prices are usually inflated.
Visit Sidi Bou Said in the late afternoon for the best light on the blue-and-white architecture.
Insist taxi drivers use the meter, or agree on a fare before departing.
Pace yourself — this itinerary includes free time so you are not rushing between every attraction.
packingList: Copies of booking confirmations and travel insurance
Lightweight, breathable clothing and a reusable water bottle
Modest clothing for the Medina and non-resort areas
Comfortable shoes for uneven Medina alleys and cobblestones
thingsToDo: Day trip to the Sahara edge near Douz (multi-day)
Coastal walks around La Marsa and Gammarth
thingsToAvoid: Paying a taxi's first quoted price without confirming the meter is on.
Wandering the Medina's quieter alleys alone after dark.
Skipping travel insurance and checking current regional travel advisories before booking.
topRestaurants: Dar El Jeld — Traditional Tunisian fine dining ($$$)
Le Golfe — Seafood, Sidi Bou Said ($$$)
instagramSpots: Sidi Bou Said blue doors and bougainvillea
adventureActivities: Day trip to the Sahara edge near Douz (multi-day)
Coastal walks around La Marsa and Gammarth
luxuryExperiences: Private guided Carthage and Sidi Bou Said tour
Suite stay at a Gammarth beach resort
Fine dining at Dar El Jeld in a restored Medina mansion
honeymoonExperiences: Sunset in Sidi Bou Said overlooking the bay
Private dinner in a converted Medina courtyard house
Beach day at a Gammarth resort
budgetExperiences: Free wander through the Medina souqs
Sidi Bou Said village (free to explore)
localEtiquette: Dress modestly, particularly in the Medina and outside beach resort areas.
Greetings are warm — a handshake is standard; close acquaintances may exchange cheek kisses.
Bargaining is customary and expected in the Medina souqs.
Friday is a significant day for prayer; some shops close for a midday period.
localTransportGuide: The TGM light rail line runs from central Tunis out to Carthage, Sidi Bou Said, and La Marsa — a scenic, easy way to reach the coastal sights. Taxis are inexpensive within the city (insist on the meter); the Medina itself is pedestrian-only.
bestTimeToVisit: March-May and September-November, for mild, comfortable temperatures.
weather: Mediterranean climate — hot, dry summers (highs around 33°C) and mild, wetter winters (highs around 15°C).
visaInformation: Visa-free entry for stays up to 90 days for most Western passport holders including the US, UK, EU, Canada, and Australia. Indian nationals generally need a visa arranged in advance — check current requirements before booking.
averageFlightTime: ~10.3h from major hubs (estimate; varies by origin — see Suggested Flights)
cancellationPolicy: Cancellations made 30+ days before departure: full refund minus a $99 processing fee. 15-29 days before departure: 50% refund. 0-14 days before departure or no-show: non-refundable. Airline and hotel supplier cancellation terms may be stricter than this policy and apply in addition — your travel consultant confirms exact terms before payment is taken. No online payment is collected on this site; a consultant confirms final pricing and availability with you directly before any booking is confirmed.
termsAndConditions: Pricing shown is per person based on the traveler type and tier selected and is an estimate pending live fare and rate confirmation.
Passports must be valid for at least 6 months beyond the return date for most destinations — confirm the exact requirement for your nationality.
Itinerary order may change due to weather, local conditions, or attraction closures; inclusions remain equivalent.
Solo travelers incur a single-room supplement, reflected in the Solo pricing tier.
This package does not include international travel insurance unless explicitly listed under Included.
Prices shown in a currency other than USD are approximate conversions at the rate noted and are not a payment quote.

faqs.question: Do I need a visa to visit Tunis?
faqs.answer: Visa-free entry for stays up to 90 days for most Western passport holders including the US, UK, EU, Canada, and Australia. Indian nationals generally need a visa arranged in advance — check current requirements before booking.

faqs.question: What is the best time to visit Tunis?
faqs.answer: March-May and September-November, for mild, comfortable temperatures. Mediterranean climate — hot, dry summers (highs around 33°C) and mild, wetter winters (highs around 15°C).

faqs.question: What's included in this package?
faqs.answer: Round-trip airfare, accommodation, daily breakfast, airport transfers, daily local transport, and guided tours to the attractions listed in the itinerary. See the Included / Not Included sections for full detail.

faqs.question: Can the itinerary be customized?
faqs.answer: Yes — this itinerary is a starting point. Your travel consultant can add, remove, or reorder activities, extend your stay, or upgrade your hotel category before you book.

faqs.question: What is the cancellation policy?
faqs.answer: Cancellations made 30+ days before departure: full refund minus a $99 processing fee. 15-29 days before departure: 50% refund. 0-14 days before departure or no-show: non-refundable. Airline and hotel supplier cancellation terms may be stricter than this policy and apply in addition — your travel consultant confirms exact terms before payment is taken. No online payment is collected on this site; a consultant confirms final pricing and availability with you directly before any booking is confirmed.

faqs.question: Is Tunis safe for tourists?
faqs.answer: Tunis is generally safe for tourists who take standard precautions — avoid poorly lit areas at night, keep valuables secure, and use licensed transport.

faqs.question: What currency should I bring to Tunis?
faqs.answer: The local currency is TND. Carry some cash for smaller vendors — card acceptance can be inconsistent outside major hotels and malls.

faqs.question: Is this package good for a honeymoon or family trip?
faqs.answer: This itinerary suits couples, solo travelers, and small groups equally well — talk to your consultant about tailoring it further.

reviews.author: Hiroshi Sharma
reviews.title: A little rushed but still wonderful
reviews.body: Sidi Bou Said day visit alone was worth the trip, but combined with Bardo National Museum this itinerary really delivered. Breakfast at the hotel was good every day and the local guide was knowledgeable.

reviews.author: Ava Williams
reviews.title: Five stars, will return
reviews.body: Medina of Tunis alone was worth the trip, but combined with Avenue Habib Bourguiba this itinerary really delivered. Breakfast at the hotel was good every day and the local guide was knowledgeable.

reviews.author: Marco van Dijk
reviews.title: Five stars, will return
reviews.body: We added a couple of optional activities beyond Carthage archaeological site and don't regret it. Good value for what was included, and the food recommendations from our guide were spot on.

reviews.author: Ethan Brown
reviews.title: Worth every penny
reviews.body: Everything was handled for us — flights, hotel, transfers, and tours to Zitouna Mosque courtyard and Sidi Bou Said village streets. Great communication from the consultant before departure too.
